description_long: This object is a classic bar-style mobile phone with a compact, rectangular form and rounded edges. The front features a small, cracked display screen above a physical keypad with raised, tactile buttons, including a central navigation button and colored call/end keys. The back is mostly plain except for a single small camera lens positioned near the top center. The sides are smooth with minimal protrusions, and the phone appears to be constructed primarily from plastic with some internal metal components. The color scheme is predominantly black on the front and white on the back, giving it a two-tone appearance. The overall design is utilitarian, typical of early 2000s feature phones, and lacks any visible branding or decorative elements.
description: A compact, two-tone bar-style mobile phone with a cracked screen, physical keypad, and a single rear camera. The design is simple and utilitarian, with black and white plastic surfaces.
description_view_0: Front view shows a cracked display screen, a full physical keypad with colored buttons, and a black faceplate.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the phone's slim profile and the color split between the black front and white back.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a plain white back and a black back with a single small camera lens near the top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the phone's thickness and the continuation of the two-tone color scheme.
attribute_screen_display_content: The screen is visibly cracked and appears to be blank, with no icons, wallpaper, or interface elements displayed.
attribute_camera_placement: A single small camera lens is located near the top center of the back of the phone.
attribute_button_layout: The front features a physical keypad with twelve main buttons arranged in a grid, including a central navigation button and colored call/end keys. There is also a small side button visible on one edge.
